observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management request the SB to pass a resolution authorizing the LCE to transfer these assets to their respective recipient barangays.
We also recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, after the completion of the required procedures and securing the necessary documents for the transfer of assets, to record the transfer in the books of the LGU as a debit to Government Equity and Accumulated Depreciation accounts relative to the specific asset being transferred and a credit to the corresponding PPE account. Consequently, record the receipt of the said assets in the books of the recipient barangay as a debit to appropriate PPE account, net of accumulated depreciation, and credit to Donations in Kind account.
We further recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review of the financial statements, property records, and inventory reports to identify any remaining PPE that are already under the custody, actual use, and overall supervision of other government agencies or barangays but are still recorded in the books of accounts of the LGU. Upon identification of such assets, transfer and derecognize them in the books of accounts following the aforementioned procedures to ensure the reliability of the PPE account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the GSO, in coordination with the Inventory Committee and other concerned personnel or committees, to expedite and prioritize the implementation of the required one-time cleansing of the LGU's PPE account balances.
We further recommended that management direct the GSO, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, the OIC – Municipal Accountant, and other concerned personnel, to adopt the procedures and documentation in accordance with the aforementioned guidelines to address the proper disposition and derecognition of missing or non-existent properties, ensuring more reliable financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Inventory and Disposal Committee of the LGU and the Appraisal Committee or an equivalent body to initiate and facilitate the reappraisal of the 22 unserviceable properties costing ₱7,588,648.00 and subject the same to Negotiated Sale in accordance with the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties; and
GSO, in coordination with the Inventory and Disposal Committee of the LGU, as well as other concerned personnel to:
Facilitate the preparation and/or finalize the IIRUP with provision of reasonable appraisal value of those unserviceable properties with ongoing disposal process and/or not yet subjected to disposal proceedings costing ₱17,342,454.00 and promptly cause the disposal of these properties taking into consideration the appropriate and most advantageous mode of disposal following the conditions and procedures provided in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties;
Submit the IIRUP duly supported with the necessary documents to the Accounting Office upon completion of disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts; and
Familiarize themselves with the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties to minimize procedural and documentation errors/lapses relative to the appraisal and disposal process of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure that all postpaid plans are properly monitored to avoid excess payments;
Verify the postpaid plan users and submit a report thereon;
Collect the total amount of ₱102,925.46 as excess payment beyond the subscription plan to responsible postpaid recipients, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer; and
Make the necessary journal entry to recognize the communication equipment under PPE and semi-expendable properties.
Require the GSO to issue ICS or PAR to all recipient of postpaid plan with communication equipment, as well as to include the mobile devices in the RPCPPE and RPCSP.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers (DVs), payrolls and other JEVs from September 2024 to December 2024 and official receipts from October to December 2024.
We also recommended that Management require the concerned accountable officers to observe the prescribed period in the submission of accounts and financial reports for timely communication of any deficiency that may be noted in audit which may be relevant in decision making.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to submit the required monthly BRS within 10 days after the receipt of snapshots and/or bank statements. If warranted, register all the bank accounts in an online banking facility/system available in the depository banks of the LGU to obtain timely bank statements.
We also recommended that Management direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to submit the required monthly pre-closing trial balance and its corresponding schedules as well as the SAAOB for each fund on or before the 20th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC Chairperson through the BAC Secretariat to submit copies of perfected contracts, approved POs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period of five working days upon perfection of contract for a timely review and evaluation thereof; and
Instruct the BAC to refrain from proceeding with the procurement process in the absence of the required documents and/or if any deficiencies are identified.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of granting significant amount of cash advances to certain employees who are not designated as SDOs and who are not bonded; instead, direct the Municipal Treasurer to issue check payable directly to the supplier and withhold the applicable taxes therefrom. If the disbursements pertain to events and/or activities regularly conducted by the LGU, include them in the annual procurement plan and purchase the same through applicable procurement method.
Exceptions may apply in cases where the needed items are highly necessary and urgent or are petty in nature requiring immediate payment thereof, or making payment through check is impractical. In these cases, we recommend to use the Petty Cash Fund for payments of petty expenses.
We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the concerned accountable officers, to immediately submit the liquidation reports pertaining to the abovementioned transactions to the Audit Team for further evaluation.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to refrain from recommending the award of contracts to bidders whose registered line of business does not align with the goods to be procured.
We also recommended that Management instruct the BAC and TWG to conduct a thorough post-qualification process by carefully reviewing the relevant permits and licenses required for the goods or services to be delivered or performed.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned officers and employees to refund the net overpayment of subsistence and laundry allowances totaling ₱15,669.13; and
Direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant with the assistance of the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to:
Henceforth ensure that subsistence and laundry allowances of MHWs are computed based on the actual number of services rendered as reflected on their approved DTRs and on prescribed rates and guidelines; and
Submit the lacking approved DTRs of some MHWs for further review and evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management direct the HRMO to stop the practice of granting requests for the monetization of leave credits in the 10-day to 30-day category solely from accrued SL credits and/or from both accumulated VL and SL credits when VL credits are insufficient. Instead, monetization should be applied exclusively to VL credits if the balance allows, provided that a minimum of five days is retained after monetization.
We also recommended that Management direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents and henceforth, ensure that the monetization of leave credits is supported with complete documentation before approving the payment thereof.

We recommended that Management direct the:
BAC and the BAC Secretariat to strictly observe the required procurement process under the “Negotiated Procurement - Lease of Real Property and Venue” mode of procurement;
OIC - Municipal Accountant:
With the assistance of BAC Secretariat, to immediately submit the aforementioned lacking documents for review and evaluation of the Audit Team; and
To closely monitor and review the DVs as to the completeness and validity of supporting documents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Cause the collection of business taxes from the foregoing contractors who engage in projects within the LGU applying the situs of taxation amounting to ₱424,110.62. In this regard, the Municipal Treasurer could deduct the same from any claims due to the concerned contractors, if any, or issue demand letters to these contractors or require them to settle the accrued business taxes, including the corresponding penalties and interest, if warranted, as a prerequisite for their participation in the future bidding of infrastructure projects of the LGU; and
Strictly observe the Municipal Ordinance by thoroughly examining the accuracy of the declared gross receipts of all taxpayers by considering all available evidence and records to ensure that tax assessment is accurate and to avoid non-collection of business taxes that should have been earned by the LGU.
We also recommended that Management direct the BPLO, in coordination with the Municipal Building Official, to strictly monitor the businesses of contractors within the LGU to ensure collection of applicable local taxes.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Require the deputized barangay collectors of community tax to remit all their collections intact following the procedures provided in the Manual on the Financial Management of Barangays; and
Stop the practice of requiring the deputized barangay collectors to pay for the cost of CTCs while ensuring compliance with the proper procedures for establishing and monitoring accountability for these accountable forms.
We also recommended that Management instruct the OIC -Municipal Accountant to properly record the full remittance of community tax and the corresponding share due to the barangays deputized by the LGU with the following pro-forma entry, as the case may be.
Cash – Local Treasury (dr)
Community Tax (50% share of the LGU) (cr)
Due to LGUs (50% share of the barangay) (cr)

1.8. We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Require the immediate liquidation of cash advances, especially those
aging one year and below, otherwise, require the employees who are
still active in service to refund the amount advanced or withhold the
same from any amount due to them;
b. Ensure that no additional cash advances be granted unless the
previous cash advance was fully liquidated; and
c. Continue to send demand letters to those employees with long
outstanding cash advances for immediate settlement.

2.9. We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant:

a. To gather the original documents and validate the accounts payable
which had been outstanding for more than two years. If found invalid
or no original supporting documents could be found, the LGU may
revert said payables to the unappropriated surplus; and
b. To ensure that all supporting documents are complete prior to
preparation of JEV to record the same and attached said original
supporting documents to the JEV for submission to the Audit Team.

3.5. We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for proper
and fair presentation of accounts in the financial for statement:

3.6. We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to transfer the funds pertaining to liquidated damages
collected under the TF amounting to ₱24,699.80 to the GF.

4.10. We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to:
• Provide the Municipal Accountant monthly report showing the
list of delinquent stallholders and their corresponding unpaid
balances; and
• Maximize their efforts in collecting delinquent rental fees and
reconsider the imposition of penalties and interest thereon to
generate additional income for further economic development of
the LGU.

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Record the unrecognized unpaid rentals in the books of the LGU
as of year-end as a debit to Operating Lease Receivable and credit
to Rent Income in the amount of ₱5,930,441.08; and
• To strictly adhere to the accrual method of accounting by properly
recognizing the unpaid balances of delinquent stallholders
including penalties and interest thereon in the books of accounts.

5.9. We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal
entries duly supported with the required documents to recognize the
accrued cost on the actual work accomplishments of the foregoing
projects as of year-end.
b. Require the Municipal Engineer to furnish copy of status report on
implementation of infrastructure projects as at year-end duly
supported with inspection reports as well as geotagged photos to the
Municipal Accountant to serve as bases in recognizing accruals in the
books of accounts.

6.7. We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or in the
NTFS for the changes in the original budget and material discrepancies
between the budget and actual amounts to enhance transparency of the
financial statements of the LGU.

7.9. We recommended that Management:

a. Restate the accounts affected by prior period errors for all ensuing
financial statements to fairly present the comparative financial
statements of the LGU; and
b. Observe the required disclosures in the NTFS relative to the recorded
prior period errors/adjustments.

We recommended that Management:

b. Require the GSO to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials to be reported in the RPCI and issue RIS in the issuance thereof.

Management instructed the Municipal Budget Officer to comply with the audit recommendation.
Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to existing laws, rules and regulations on the proper handling of collections and deposits and ensure that collections are deposited intact daily or not later than the next banking day with the government depository bank of the agency to avoid loss through theft, misuse or misapplication of government funds.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: Management assured compliance of the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer, OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the LGU’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of cash deficit.
We further recommended that Management devise and implement cost-cutting measures to eliminate the cash deficit of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and property regularly and prepare and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deduct/withhold the salaries of the municipal officials and employees still in service who have unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all accounts be submitted on or before the 10th day of the following month to the auditor for timely review and examination of financial transactions of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the BAC Secretariat to strictly observe the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 in order to attain government policy of competitiveness, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process; and
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to require the submission of all documentary requirements and ensure that all pertinent documents are dated before processing the payments.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure the submission of the contracts, POs and its supporting documents within five working days from the execution of contracts for a systematic process and timely review as to completeness of the requirements.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:
Strictly adhere with the provisions on the requirements and procurement procedures in order to attain the government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process; and
Ensure that reference to brand names shall not be allowed in the procurement process except those prescribed under Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to impose the liquidated damages totaling ₱74,219.79 to the concerned suppliers, as well as, to the supplier of construction materials for municipal museum and collect the same by deducting from any money due them or which may become due to them.
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents within five working days from the execution thereof for timely review of the contracts and supporting documents.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:
Strictly adhere to the provisions on the requirements and procurement procedures set forth in 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to attain the government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process;
Ensure that in every procurement, the RFQ must indicate the ABC in order to effectively obtain the most advantageous price for the LGU; and
Ensure competitiveness and equal opportunities to all potential bidders by allowing them to provide their offers and not giving RFQs with readily printed price quotations.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:
Strictly adhere to the provisions on the requirements and procurement procedures in order to attain the government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement; and
Ensure the consistency of contract duration posted in the advertising and contents of the Bid Notice Abstract and POs.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:
Strictly adhere to the provisions on the requirements and procurement procedures set forth in 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to attain the government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement; and
Thoroughly evaluate the bids, particularly on identification of the approved budget in every procurement including the cost of labor to ensure that the bid price would not exceed the ABC.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:
Strictly adhere to the provisions on the requirements and procurement procedures particularly in the use of alternative mode of procurement–negotiated procurement through adjacent or contiguous modality in order to attain the government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process;
Ensure that variation orders must be within the threshold set forth in Section 1.4, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Ensure that all procurements are within the APP in order to effectively maintain efficiency, effectiveness and transparency of the procurement process.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Management instruct the BAC to:
Stop the practice of procuring goods and services from the employees’ cooperative pursuant to the afore-cited law, rules and regulations; and
Strictly comply with the requirements of RA No. 7160 and GPPB Circular No. 08-2017 in order to attain government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to continue of exerting efforts in the collection of delinquent Stall Rental Fees and Real Property Taxes (RPT), in order to generate additional income of the Municipality which could be used to implement more projects beneficial to its constituents.


	We further recommended, in relation to the collection of delinquent RPT, to seek the assistance of Barangay Officials in notifying and follow-up RPT payment of the taxpayers. If payments were not collected, proceed to the remedies provided by law through administrative or judicial action as enumerated in Section 174 of Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Market Supervisor and the Municipal Treasurer intensified their collection through serving Demand Letters, Notices of Delinquency and Statement of Accounts.


The Municipal Treasurer assured to adhere to the recommendation. Staffs of the Municipal Treasury Office regularly conducts dissemination of tax information to barangays and notices of delinquency were already publish in newspapers.
observations.reason:
Verified that as of December 31, 2020, delinquent stall rentals amounting to ₱342,090.65 remained uncollected and the delinquent RPT amounted to ₱276,435,916.19.

Not Implemented

Verified the actions taken by the Municipality to collect delinquent RPTs, however, civil remedies were not completely adhered with in order to collect the delinquent RPTs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify the composition of the Land account of the LGU recorded in the books to determine the validity in the recording of the lot property with land area of 31,000 square meters and if found to be included in the land account in the previous year, prepare adjusting entry to correct the journal entry made; and
In case the land was not previously recorded in the books, request the Provincial Appraisal Committee to reappraise the value of the lot property to reflect the accurate fair market value of the said property and direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to adjust the recorded value of the Land account in order to fairly present the recorded balance of the PPE account in the Statement of Financial Position.
We recommended that Management close the dormant savings account with PPSB and transfer the funds to the LBP account under Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account maintained by the LGU.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal GSO and Head of the Appraisal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration and loss of value of the properties and generate additional funds from the sale thereof.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to remit the unexpended balances of Due to NGAs account to the respective Source Agencies or to the National Treasury as additional resources to be used for other developmental projects of the national government.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure all collection officers remit their collections to the Treasury Office within the prescribed period in compliance with Section 63 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I to safeguard government funds against possible loss or misuse; and
Require the various collection officers to strictly adhere to the regulations by remitting their collections to the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer at the close of each business day in order to avoid accumulation of cash in their possession for unreasonable length of time.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to existing laws, rules and regulations on the proper handling of collections and deposits and ensure that collections are deposited intact daily or not later than the next banking day with the government depository bank of the agency to avoid loss through theft, misuse or misapplication of government funds.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documents to support the payments made to the various suppliers and ensure that all DVs are properly and duly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairman to stop the practice of procuring goods and services from the employees’ cooperative pursuant to the afore-cited law, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that Management require the BAC to strictly comply with the requirements of RA No. 7160 and Section 5.0 of GPPB Circular 08-2017 in order to attain government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to consolidate the PPMP into an APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of various office supplies and equipment and to ensure that procurement is done in an efficient, effective and economical manner that is most advantageous to the LGU and in order to promote transparency in the procurement process aimed at reducing procurement costs.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC and the Municipal GSO to strictly adhere to the requirements and procurement procedure set forth in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to attain the government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of funds and strictly observe the policies and guidelines on the documentary requirements under Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and to submit the lacking documentary requirements to establish the validity of the claims;
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 3 of DBM BC No. 2007-3 and ensure that the amount necessary for payment of honoraria shall be sourced and authorized under BC 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005; and
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to require the BAC and TWG members to refund the honoraria charged against the OMOE, EME and Other General Services in the total amount of ₱592,630.00.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to inform the SB Members to stop the practice of exercising the power to implement programs, projects and activities pertaining to the procurement of office and disinfectant supplies from issuing RFQs, Inspection and Acceptance wherein  it is         the main functions of the BAC and the General Services Office and not the Office of the SB; and
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documents to support the payments made to the supplier and ensure that all DVs are properly and duly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management:
Seek the authorization from the SB for each and every project with no specific allotted amount as an authority in its implementation of PPAs pursuant to  Sections 22 (c) and 447 (2) (i) of RA No. 7160; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to allot an amount of each project in the budget under the Office of the Municipal Mayor identified in the object of expenditures such as financial assistance and capital outlay - infrastructure projects before submission to the SB for review and approval pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO), to require all regular and casual employees submit their DTR monthly to support the payment of the salaries and wages payroll.

We recommended that Management instruct the HRMO with the assistance of the OIC-Municipal Accountant to certify only the loan applications of employees whose salary could accommodate the monthly loan amortization and would still have a net take home pay of not less than the amount specified in the General Provisions of GAA of 2020.

We recommended that Management stop granting rice subsidy to the national employees of national government agencies in addition to the rice allowance received from their respective agencies which resulted in the additional food supplies expense to the LGU.  Also, we recommended that management adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds.

We recommended that Management instruct the LGU PBAC to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to support the contract with the proponent and for technical review and evaluation, and to ensure that public-private partnership project is properly and duly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management ensure collection of the delinquent accounts on realty taxes through the Municipal Treasurer by adopting the procedures and remedies provided in Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160 to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the LGU geared towards the welfare of the constituents.

We recommended that Management direct the Market Supervisor and Municipal Treasurer to continue to intensify efforts in the collection of overdue rentals from delinquent market stall leaseholders.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Market Supervisor to issue Statement of Accounts at least a month before the rent becomes due to ensure prompt payment of rentals.

We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to appropriate and utilize the SEF in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned Joint Circulars and RA No. 7160 and discontinue the incurrence of expenditures which are not related thereof. The Local Chief Executive, as the head of the Local School Board, should strictly adhere to the regulation in the prioritization and utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that Management:
Reorganize the Municipal Local Solid Waste Management Board to include the Chairperson of the SK Federation and representative from the NGOs whose principal purpose is to promote recycling and the protection of air and water quality in order that the required representation pursuant to RA No. 9003 is complied and representatives from the SK Federation and NGOs will be included in the formulation and implementation of the solid waste management plans and programs of the LGU and to acknowledge the role of youth as one of the key influencers in environment advocacy; and
Instruct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to require all the 66 barangays to maintain their respective MRFs as mandated under RA No. 9003 to ensure proper disposal of solid waste towards protection of public health and its environment.

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all road networks, prepare and submit complete Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) to the Municipal Accounting Office and GSO to serve as reference in the reconciliation of Inventory Report with the accounting and GSO records;
observations.managementAction: Physical count of local road network was completed and the RPCLRN was prepared and submitted by the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO.
observations.reason: RCPLRN was already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue charging expenses not related to disaster mitigation, prevention, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery and be more prudent in the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the said fund.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) together with the duplicate copies of the Official Receipt which shall be the basis of the Accounting Unit in the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher and recording  in the Cash Receipts Journal as prescribed in the provisions of Sections 29 and 35 of  Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management
instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of
all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and to reconcile the
Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the
accounting records.

We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services
Officer maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment ledger card and property
card, respectively. We further recommended the Management to consider the use of
PPE Asset Management Software to facilitate the inventory recording and tagging of
the LGU’s PPE.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all road
networks, prepare and submit complete Report on Physical Count of
Local Road Network (RPCLRN) to the Municipal Accounting Office
and General Services Office (GSO) to serve as reference in the
reconciliation of Inventory Report with the accounting and GSO
records;
b) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for all existing
road projects to facilitate recording in the books and the preparation of
subsidiary ledgers for road and its components;
c) Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services
Officer to maintain complete and updated Local Road Network Ledger
Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively, for each
road component of every road network;
d) Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation cost for
the road networks account and to verify the recorded road networks
previously carried in the Registry of Public Infrastructures if these were
included in the inventory and still existing, and to make the necessary
adjustment in the books to reflect the correct balance of the account; and
e) Ensure the implementation of the accounting and reporting guidelines
of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 5, 2015.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
strictly comply with the provision of Section 32 of MNGAS, which requires that
collections shall be deposited intact with the authorized depository bank daily or
not later than the next banking day to avoid possible loss through theft, misuse or
misapplication of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue to issue demand
letters to municipal officials and employees concerned to remind them
of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances, and
employees who were already resigned or transferred to other office, and
to cause the liquidation or settlement of their unliquidated balances;
b) Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the
required supporting documents in the processing of claims in the
granting of cash advances;
c) Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting
additional cash advance to official and employee unless the previous
cash advance granted has been liquidated;
d) Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the
submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof to
avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and
e) Require the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of the
municipal officials and employees concerned with unliquidated cash
advances pursuant to Section 9.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004
considering that they have been notified and given more adequate time
to liquidate their cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Budget Officer to discontinue charging expenses not related to disaster mitigation,
prevention, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery and be more
prudent in the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the
utilization of the said fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose the Liquidated
Damages to the Supplier concerned in the amount of ₱87,762.50 in addition to the
₱60,987.50 previously deducted from his claim due to incurrence of 113 days of
delay in the delivery of the item purchased, equivalent to 10% of the contract price.

We recommended that the OIC- Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of 2019
QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund books for easy monitoring of the
utilization and to ensure the availability of funds to support programs and activities
under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Instruct the BAC Secretariat and the OIC - Municipal Accountant to
submit all the lacking documents to the Audit Team to support the
payment made to the supplier and for technical evaluation and
inspection, and to ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly
and duly supported with complete documentation;
2. Require the BAC TWG and BAC Secretariat to submit written
justification/explanation relative to the noted lapses and provide
additional information/ documentation so as to ascertain the validity of
47
the post-qualification of the process conducted. Henceforth, the BAC
TWG should be more vigilant in the conduct and scrutiny of the
documents being submitted by the contractor to ensure compliance with
the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR;
3. Direct the Municipal Officials concerned to require supplier/contractor
additional performance security in case the Certificate of Authority
issued by the Insurance Commission to transact insurance business will
no longer be valid at the time of purchased; and
4. Require the Municipal Officials concerned to strictly observe the
provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, Section 9.1 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 and provisions of RA 9184 to establish validity of the claim.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Executive instruct
the OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to
submit complete set of monthly disbursement vouchers together with its supporting
documents and collection accounts within the first ten (10) days of the following
month for timely verification of the financial transactions.

We also recommended that the Management require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers and collection accounts together
with the suppporting documents and official receipts as shown in Annex F-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity
the Procurement and Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB
pursuant to 2016 IRR of RA 9184.

We further recommended that the BAC furnish the audit team copy of the
said report to facilitate monitoring on the status of the procurement process
undertaken and basis for the contract review on the procured goods and
infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC
Secretariat to submit the perfected contracts to the COA Audit Team within the
prescribed period for timely review and evaluation thereof pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that Management:
a) Continue to intensify the Solid Waste Management information
dissemination campaign and enforce all regulations to the extent of
imposing penalties for non-compliance therewith; and
b) Consider to develop its own sanitary landfill for future disposal of
residual non-recyclable wastes for continuous delivery of efficient and
effective service to its constituents and protection of public health and
environment.

We recommended that Management ensure that Gender and Development
(GAD) Plan of the agency will fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and
objectives towards the role of women in becoming a participative partner in nation
building and development with the end view of protecting their lives and rights and
to live a modest life in a society.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
continue of exerting efforts in the collection of delinquent Stall Rental Fees and
Real Property Taxes (RPT), in order to generate additional income of the
Municipality which could be used to implement more projects beneficial to its
constituents.

We further recommended, in relation to the collection of delinquent RPT, to
seek the assistance of Barangay Officials in notifying and follow-up RPT payment
of the taxpayers. If payments were not collected, proceed to the remedies provided
by law through administrative or judicial action as enumerated in Section 174 of
Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended that Management continue to collect and remit taxes due
the national government on time. Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office is
advised to be vigilant so as not to incur under/over-remittance of taxes withheld.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 dated July 9, 2013, COA Circular No. 2004-008 dated September 9, 2004 and COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 to ensure that cashbooks are updated and certified by the Municipal Treasurer and reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Complied with. ✓

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council thru the MDRRM Officer to formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP and carry out the programs, projects and activities identified therein to utilize the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the Local GAD Focal Point Team to establish the GAD Database and to provide necessary resources/equipment to facilitate the creation of the sex-disaggregated database which serves as a basis for a gender-responsive planning, program and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: Established and updated the GAD Data Base of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to collect and remit taxes due the national government on time. Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office is advised to be vigilant so as not to incur under/over-remittance of taxes withheld.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant and OIC - General Services Officer to reconcile their records to determine the discrepancies noted, and make the necessary adjustments thereof.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 dated July 9, 2013, COA Circular No. 2004-008 dated September 9, 2004 and COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 to ensure that cashbooks are updated and certified by the Municipal Treasurer and reconciled with the accounting records.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management instruct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to demand for the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances by issuing final demand letters to the persons concerned and if no liquidations have been made, impose the sanction of withholding the salaries of those who still fail to settle their accounts after due notice.

We further recommended that Management require the OIC - Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to monitor strictly the settlement of unliquidated cash advances and stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to municipal officials and employees until prior advances have been liquidated. Subsequently, officials and employees with outstanding cash advances who resigned or retired from the service should be required to settle/liquidate their cash advances before issuing the clearance from money and property accountability.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council thru the MDRRM Officer to formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP and carry out the programs, projects and activities identified therein to utilize the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the Local GAD Focal Point Team to establish the GAD Database and to provide necessary resources/equipment to facilitate the creation of the sex-disaggregated database which serves as a basis for a gender-responsive planning, program and policy formulation.

We recommended that Management exert effort to collect the stall rentals and send final demand letters to all the delinquent stallholders. If payments of market stall rentals are not collected from the stallholders, proceed to the remedies as provided by law through administrative or judicial action as enumerated in Section 174 of Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended that Management continue to collect and remit taxes due the national government on time. Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office is advised to be vigilant so as not to incur under/over-remittance of taxes withheld.

We recommended that Management immediately enforce the settlement of all audit disallowances in compliance with Sections 5.4 and 7.1.1, Chapter II of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Likewise, we enjoined Management to be more vigilant in the implementation of the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing of claims to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of inventory taking of all types of PPE of the Municipality to determine their existence and condition and to reconcile the amount in the inventory report with the balance in the Equipment Ledger Card/ general ledger in order to arrive at the correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Likewise, we recommended that management require the OIC - General Services Officer to expedite the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor in compliance to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS, Vol. I.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted to the Audit Team copy of Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.reason: RCPPE submitted to the Audit Team contains unreconciled PPE with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management strictly adhere with the provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184 and  create an Inspectorate Team or Project Monitoring Committee to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of implemented projects with the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning Development Officer (MPDO) as mandatory members.
